suppressed_rules.reason: The newer rule preserves the event mechanism while separating beneficial mitigation from the outcome, covering the older non-binary terminal distinction more precisely.
The newer rule keeps the object and institution test while adding transferable-function separation and a stricter decision procedure.
The newer rule operationalizes career specificity through service object, institution, qualification, and income endpoints.
The newer family rule retains multi-axis atomization and adds independent closure for role, absence cause, substitute carer, health, migration, and endpoint.
The newer rule supersedes the same method-versus-service-object distinction with explicit institution, qualification, timing, and income checks.
The newer rule retains activation-versus-completion separation and extends it into an auditable relationship-complexity ladder.
The newer rule includes capacity-versus-current-status separation and requires independent evidence for each additional relationship stage.
The newer rule preserves the multistage burden and adds a concrete rollback procedure when a stage lacks evidence.
The newer rule keeps mechanism-versus-outcome separation and directly prevents beneficial signals from cancelling an otherwise complete event mechanism.
